#+TITLE: Block Edge Cases

* Matched blocks

#+BEGIN_SRC python
def hello():
    print("hello")
#+END_SRC

#+BEGIN_SRC rust
fn main() {
    println!("hello");
}
#+END_SRC

#+BEGIN_EXAMPLE
This is example text.
It preserves formatting.
#+END_EXAMPLE

#+BEGIN_QUOTE
This is a quote block.
#+END_QUOTE

#+BEGIN_VERSE
This is verse.
  Indentation matters here.
#+END_VERSE

#+BEGIN_CENTER
Centered text.
#+END_CENTER

#+BEGIN_COMMENT
This is a comment. Not exported.
#+END_COMMENT

#+BEGIN_EXPORT html
<div>raw html</div>
#+END_EXPORT

* Lowercase blocks

#+begin_src python
code
#+end_src

#+begin_example
example
#+end_example

* Mixed case (should still work)

#+Begin_Src python
code
#+End_Src

* SRC block without language (lint warning)

#+BEGIN_SRC
some code without language
#+END_SRC

* Unclosed blocks (lint errors)

#+BEGIN_SRC python
this block never closes

* Another unclosed

#+BEGIN_QUOTE
never closed

* Unmatched END

#+END_SRC

* Nested blocks (unusual but syntactically valid in some contexts)

#+BEGIN_QUOTE
#+BEGIN_SRC python
code inside quote
#+END_SRC
#+END_QUOTE

* Block with org-mode syntax inside (should be protected)

#+BEGIN_SRC org
* This is not a heading
| not | a | table |
:PROPERTIES:
:ID: not-a-property
:END:
#+END_SRC

* Unknown block type

#+BEGIN_FOOBAR
unknown content
#+END_FOOBAR

* Empty blocks

#+BEGIN_SRC python
#+END_SRC

#+BEGIN_EXAMPLE
#+END_EXAMPLE

* Block with special characters in content

#+BEGIN_SRC bash
echo "#+END_SRC"  # tricky line that looks like block end
echo '* not a heading'
#+END_SRC

* SRC block with header arguments

#+BEGIN_SRC python :results output :exports both
print("hello")
#+END_SRC

#+BEGIN_SRC elisp :var x=5
(message "%d" x)
#+END_SRC

* Dynamic blocks

#+BEGIN: clocktable :maxlevel 3
content
#+END:

* Block at end of file without trailing newline
#+BEGIN_SRC python
code
#+END_SRC
